description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a structured, auto-fillable CRA Annex VII technical file with exportable compliance documents,
*so that* I can assemble and maintain the regulator-facing technical documentation without starting from a blank page.

h3. Scope
8-section Annex VII editor + Annex I Part I checklist; cross-product overview; 37-template document library; deterministic Batch Fill wizard; EU DoC + CVD Markdown export; PDF→Markdown migration.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the editor, *when* used, *then* all 8 Annex VII sections autosave with author/last-updated metadata ({{routes/technical-files.test.ts}}).
* *Given* the Batch Fill wizard, *when* run, *then* it auto-populates 4 sections + obligation evidence non-destructively.
* *Given* the DoC/CVD exports, *when* downloaded, *then* they return Markdown.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-125…CRAN-130 (editor → overview → templates → batch fill → exports → PDF→MD)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* an 8-section Annex VII technical-file editor with an Annex I Part I checklist,
*so that* I can author every required section of the technical file in one structured place.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/technical-files.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* per-product section CRUD is covered.
* *Given* {{technical_file_sections}}, *when* inspected, *then* {{(org_id, product_id, section_key)}} is unique.
* *Given* E2E {{technical-files.spec.ts}}, *then* section edit is covered.

h3. Implementation Notes
8 sections (Product Description → Change Management); per-section autosave + author/last-updated metadata; Annex I Part I checklist on the Compliance tab.

h3. Sources
Commit {{ceddd7c}} (2026-02-21) + Phase 2 obligation-engine commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* a cross-product technical-file overview with deep links,
*so that* I can see tech-file progress across all products and jump straight to a section.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/technical-files-overview.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* the rollup is covered.
* *Given* {{/products/<id>?tab=technical-file&section=risk-assessment}}, *when* opened, *then* it lands correctly.

h3. Implementation Notes
Summary stats + filter buttons + per-product deep links.

h3. Sources
Commit {{a4f8a45}} (2026-02-22, overview concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a library of compliance document templates auto-populated from my data,
*so that* I can generate DoC, CVD policy, post-market plan and more without rewriting boilerplate.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/document-templates.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.
* *Given* {{document_templates}}, *when* seeded, *then* the full list (37 templates) is visible on the Templates page.

h3. Implementation Notes
Auto-population from org/product/SBOM data; help-guide route ch4_05.

h3. Sources
P6 #35/#36/#37 phase commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a Batch Fill wizard that deterministically auto-populates technical-file sections,
*so that* I can pre-fill the tech file from existing data without losing any edits I've already made.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/batch-fill.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* the auto-population pipeline is covered.
* *Given* the help guide {{ch4_05}}, *then* it has 6 stations + Beck-map JSON.
* *Given* pre-existing section content, *when* batch-filled, *then* it is never overwritten without explicit re-confirm.

h3. Implementation Notes
6-station wizard; auto-fills 4 Annex VII sections + obligation evidence.

h3. Sources
Session 54 ch4_05 + auto-fill commits ({{routes/technical-file/batch-fill.ts}}).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* to download the EU DoC and CVD policy as Markdown,
*so that* I get auditable, diffable compliance documents from the technical-file tab.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{GET .../declaration-of-conformity/pdf}}, *when* called, *then* it returns {{text/markdown}} with a {{.md}} filename.
* *Given* {{GET .../cvd-policy/pdf}}, *when* called, *then* ditto.
* *Given* the buttons, *when* viewed, *then* they read '(MD)' not 'PDF'.

h3. Implementation Notes
Route path kept as {{/pdf}} for URL stability; visual-PDF rendering is a separate planned project.

h3. Sources
Commit {{b81b52e}} (2026-05-18 12:00).

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* the six PDF generators replaced by Markdown emitters and pdfkit removed,
*so that* compliance output is auditable and diffable, and a heavy dependency is gone.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{backend/package.json}} at HEAD, *when* grepped, *then* {{pdfkit}} is absent.
* *Given* the 6 former PDF endpoints, *when* called, *then* they return {{text/markdown}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Markdown pipes cleanly into downstream tools; visual PDF deferred to real demand.

h3. Sources
#45 PDF→MD migration commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer hit by an actively-exploited vulnerability,
*I want* a three-stage CRA Art. 14 notification workflow with attestation and deadline tracking,
*so that* I can discharge my 24h/72h/14d ENISA-CSIRT reporting duty with an independently-witnessed audit trail.

h3. Scope
Three-stage workflow (Early Warning / Notification / Final Report) with CSIRT country + TLP; submit-with-authorisation + RFC 3161 attestation (P10d); per-product regulatory-state countdown (P10c); smart deadline alerts (P1 #8).

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/cra-reports.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* each stage's draft + submit is covered.
* *Given* a stage submit, *when* authorised, *then* user+timestamp+UA+IP and an RFC 3161 token are co-stored.
* *Given* an approaching deadline, *when* it nears, *then* alerts fire at the documented offsets.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-131 Three-stage workflow
* CRAN-132 Submit-with-authorisation + RFC3161 (P10d)
* CRAN-133 Regulatory-state view (P10c)
* CRAN-361 Smart deadline alerts (P1 #8)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a three-stage Art. 14 notification workflow with per-stage deadlines,
*so that* I can file the Early Warning, Notification and Final Report against their statutory clocks.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/cra-reports.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* each stage's draft + submit is covered.
* *Given* {{cra_reports}}, *when* inspected, *then* it has {{awareness_at}}, {{early_warning_at}}, {{notification_at}}, {{final_report_at}}, {{csirt_country}}, {{tlp}}.
* *Given* E2E {{reports.spec.ts}}, *then* create-from-finding is covered.

h3. Implementation Notes
Early Warning (24h) / Notification (72h) / Final (14d–1m); CSIRT country selection; TLP RED/AMBER/GREEN/CLEAR.

h3. Sources
Commit {{624eb8e}} (2026-02-24 10:44).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* stage submissions to capture who authorised them plus an RFC 3161 timestamp,
*so that* every Art. 14 submission carries an independently-witnessed record of who authorised what, and when.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/submission-attestation.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* attestation capture + token co-store are covered.
* *Given* a stage submit, *when* authorised, *then* {{cra_reports.attestation_user_id}} + {{attestation_rfc3161_token}} are populated.

h3. Implementation Notes
Captures user + timestamp + UA + IP and co-stores an RFC 3161 token (P10d).

h3. Sources
Commit {{290d85f}} (2026-05-06 10:29).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a regulatory-state panel showing my live Art. 14 deadline countdown,
*so that* I can see the current stage, deadline and time remaining at a glance on the product page.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/regulatory-state.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.
* *Given* the panel, *when* rendered, *then* countdown chips (24h/72h/14d) update live.

h3. Implementation Notes
Surfaces the active Art. 14 track with stage + deadline + time-remaining (P10c).

h3. Sources
Commit {{fe1ba49}} (2026-05-06 10:13).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* scheduled alerts as each Art. 14 deadline approaches,
*so that* I'm warned ahead of every statutory cutoff rather than discovering it late.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/alert-emails.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* deadline-approaching emails are covered.
* *Given* the scheduler, *when* it runs, *then* alerts fire at T-12h (24h), T-24h (72h), T-72h (14d).

h3. Implementation Notes
Routed to stakeholders via the standard notification channels (P1 #8).

h3. Sources
P1 #8 commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* a role-aware obligations engine that maps every CRA obligation onto every product,
*so that* I always know which of my obligations apply, their status, and how that status is derived from live evidence.

h3. Scope
Obligations table + cross-product tracker; 35-obligation registry (manufacturer/importer/distributor/OSS steward); role-aware applicability + derivation ({{effective = max(manual, derived)}}); batched ensure; end-of-life calculator; EU-AR decision tree.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/obligations.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* list/update/status transitions are covered.
* *Given* {{services/obligation-engine-roles.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* 35 obligations + role mapping are verified.
* *Given* a manual {{met}} vs derived {{not_started}}, *then* the manual status is preserved.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-134…CRAN-137 + CRAN-362 (EoL calculator) + CRAN-363 (EU-AR decision tree)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* an obligations table and cross-product tracker with inline status,
*so that* I can track each product's obligations and update their status in place.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/obligations.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* list/update/status transitions are covered.
* *Given* {{obligations}}, *when* inspected, *then* {{(org_id, product_id, obligation_key)}} is unique at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
Status {{not_started}}/{{in_progress}}/{{met}}; inline dropdown on product detail; {{ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING}} keeps ensure-paths idempotent.

h3. Sources
Commit {{a4f8a45}} (2026-02-22, obligations concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* a registry of all 35 CRA obligations tagged by category and role,
*so that* the engine knows the full obligation set and which roles each applies to.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/obligation-engine-roles.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* 35 obligations enumerate with role-applicability verified.
* *Given* {{CraRole}}, *when* inspected, *then* it is {{manufacturer|importer|distributor|open_source_steward}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
19 manufacturer (Art. 13/14/16/20/32/Annexes) + 10 importer (Art. 18) + 6 distributor (Art. 19); each carries {{appliesTo}} + {{appliesToRoles}}; OSS stewards share the manufacturer set.

h3. Sources
Phase 2 + role-aware commits ({{services/obligation-engine.ts}}).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* role-aware applicability filtering and live status derivation,
*so that* obligation status reflects my actual product/SBOM/scan/tech-file state, never overriding my manual entries.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{integration/role-specific-obligations.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* each role's derivation path is covered.
* *Given* E2E {{role-aware-obligations.spec.ts}}, *then* it passes.
* *Given* a manual {{met}} vs derived {{not_started}}, *then* the manual status is preserved.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{getApplicableObligations(category, role?)}} + {{computeDerivedStatuses(...)}}; {{effectiveStatus = max(manual, derived)}}.

h3. Sources
Role-aware obligations commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* batched obligation upserts chunked under Postgres parameter limits,
*so that* bulk obligation ensures are efficient and safe at scale.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/obligation-engine.ts}}, *when* inspected, *then* {{ensureObligationsBatch}} exports with chunking (500).
* *Given* the codebase, *when* grepped, *then* 10 call sites use it.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{craRole}} flows from the org's Neo4j {{craRole}} property.

h3. Sources
Role-aware obligations commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* an end-of-life notification calculator for the end-of-support obligation,
*so that* I know when I must notify customers ahead of a product's end-of-support.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* an end-of-life date, *when* set, *then* the calculator surfaces the customer-notice deadline on the product detail page.
* *Given* the calculator output, *when* computed, *then* {{end-of-support-notice}} obligation derivation reflects it.

h3. Implementation Notes
CRA Art. 13(8) end-of-support helper (#54).

h3. Sources
#54 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer unsure of their CRA role,
*I want* an interactive decision tree for whether I need an EU Authorised Representative,
*so that* I get a deterministic recommendation that also drives obligation derivation.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a fixed set of answers, *when* the tree runs, *then* it produces a deterministic recommendation.
* *Given* the recommendation, *when* made, *then* {{authorised_representative_appointed}} derivation reflects it.

h3. Implementation Notes
Walks EU-established? / Manufacturer? / Distributor? / Importer? / OSS Steward? (Art. 17, #55).

h3. Sources
#55 commits.
